summary: This is a review of 'Explanations', a follow-up book by the anonymous author of Vestiges of the Natural History of Creation, in which the author defends his theory of organic development and natural law against scientific critics. The reviewer examines key contested points — the nebular hypothesis, geological evidence for progressive species development, and spontaneous generation experiments — while remaining skeptical but acknowledging the theory's thought-provoking merits.
title: Explanations: a Sequel to “Vestiges of the Natural History of Creation.
